CheckTitle: AI Agent Must Fully Validate TLS Certificates on All HTTPS Calls
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Network Security**. Verifies that every AI agent fully validates TLS certificates — including certificate chain, hostname matching, and expiry — on all outbound HTTPS calls. Disabled or partial certificate validation makes TLS encryption worthless against a network-level adversary.
Risk: If an agent disables TLS certificate validation (e.g., verify=False, InsecureSkipVerify), a network attacker can present a self-signed certificate and intercept all HTTPS traffic, exfiltrating credentials, API keys, and sensitive payloads sent by the agent.

Code.Other:
1. Set validates_tls_certificates: true in the agent manifest.
2. Remove any verify=False, ssl_verify=False, or InsecureSkipVerify=true settings from the agent's HTTP client configuration.
3. Use a trusted CA bundle and ensure certificates are renewed before expiry using automated certificate management (e.g., cert-manager, ACM).

Recommendation.Text: Enable full TLS certificate validation in the agent's HTTP client. Never disable verification in production. Use automated certificate management to prevent expiry-related failures that tempt developers to skip validation.

Notes: ASPM-043 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
